response:
Emily has 24 residents who are divided into groups of 6, resulting in 4 groups total. Each group receives 3 initial writing prompts, so the total number of initial prompts is 4 groups × 3 prompts = 12 prompts. Additionally, Emily has 10 extra prompts to be shared equally among the 4 groups, which means each group will receive 10 ÷ 4 = 2.5 prompts. Since prompts must be whole numbers, Emily will need to round up to ensure each group receives at least 3 extra prompts. This means she needs to prepare 4 groups × 3 extra prompts = 12 extra prompts. 

In total, Emily needs to prepare 12 initial prompts + 12 extra prompts = 24 writing prompts. The library of prompts will need to be carefully curated to ensure variety and engagement. The library should also be accessible to all participants, allowing them to explore different themes and ideas. The END.
